summary: Deterministic proof for the current CLI guide claim that `cautilus discover claims` should be used before eval fixture authoring to inventory declared behavior claims and route them into human review, deterministic proof, Cautilus eval, scenario design, or alignment work.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: CLI guide claim discovery proof routing
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the current docs/guides/cli.md claim-discovery routing guidance at line 122 is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: The binary enforces that every operator runs claim discovery before manually writing eval fixtures.
The claim discovery command writes host-owned eval fixtures.
Heuristic classification is a final verdict that the repo is correct.
The focused tests prove every future natural-language claim will be classified perfectly.

notableAssertions: discover claims emits a cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1 packet with source-ref-backed claim candidates
fixture claims include human-auditable, deterministic, and cautilus-eval recommendedProof routes
classifier coverage includes ready deterministic proof, Cautilus eval, needs-scenario behavior, blocked human-auditable work, and needs-alignment ownership or surface claims
status summaries include action buckets for agent-add-deterministic-proof, agent-plan-cautilus-eval, agent-design-scenario, human-align-surfaces, human-confirm-or-decompose, and split-or-defer
review-input preparation can focus the human-align-surfaces action bucket without hand-filtering raw JSON
the public `discover claims` CLI writes a cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1 packet
the output packet includes source-ref-backed claim candidates
the CLI proof plan includes human-auditable, deterministic, and cautilus-eval recommendedProof routes

decision.reason: Focused runtime and app tests verify the public `discover claims` command and underlying classifier produce a source-referenced proof plan with deterministic, Cautilus-eval, human-auditable, scenario-design, and alignment action routes before any eval fixture writer is involved.
